questions.explanation: Given curve is {tex}{\left( {\frac{x}{a}} \right)^n} + {\left( {\frac{y}{b}} \right)^n} = 2{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow n{ \left( { \frac { x }{ a } } \right) ^{ n-1 } }\frac { 1 }{ a } +n{ \left( { \frac { y }{ b } } \right) ^{ n-1 } }\frac { 1 }{ b } \frac { { dy } }{ { dx } } =0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac { { dy } }{ { dx } } =\frac { { -b } }{ a } { \left( { \frac { { xb } }{ { ya } } } \right) ^{ n-1 } }{/tex} Hence, Slope of tangent at (a,b)= {tex}\frac{-b}{a}{/tex} Therefore, equation of tangent at (a,b) is ( y - b)= {tex}\frac{-b}{a}(x-a){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a(y - b) = -b(x - a) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} bx + ay = 2ab {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}=2{/tex}

questions.question_text: An edge of a variable cube is increasing at the rate of 3cm/sec. Find the rate at which the volume of the cube is increasing when the edge is 10cm long.
questions.question_html: An edge of a variable cube is increasing at the rate of 3cm/sec. Find the rate at which the volume of the cube is increasing when the edge is 10cm long.
questions.explanation: Let the side of the cube is x Given {tex} \frac { dx }{ dt } =3cm/sec{/tex} We have volume of a cube = V = x 3 Now, {tex}\frac { dV }{ dt } =\frac { d }{ dt } \left( { x }^{ 3 } \right) =\frac { d }{ dx } \left( { x }^{ 3 } \right) \frac { dx }{ dt } =3{ x }^{ 2 }.\frac { dx }{ dt } =9{ x }^{ 2 }{/tex} When x = 10 cm then {tex}\frac { dV }{ dt } =\quad 9.{ \left( 10 \right) }^{ 2 }=900{ cm }^{ 3 }/sec{/tex} . Which is the required solution.

questions.explanation: If a differentiable function f(x) has a relative minimum at x= 0, we have f'(0) = 0 and f'(0) > 0 Now, consider the given function y = f(x) + ax +b For this function to have a relative minimum at x = 0, we have y 1 (0) = 0 and y 2 (0) > 0 Now, {tex}{ y_{ 1 } }(0)=f'(0)+a{/tex} and {tex}{ y_{ 2 } }(0)=f''(0)>0{/tex} Since, y 1 (0) = 0, we get f'(0) + a = 0 implies a = 0

questions.question_html: Let f (x) be differentiable in (0, 4) and f (2) = f (3) and S = {c : 2 < c < 3, f’ (c) = 0 } then

questions.question_text: Find the approximate value of f(2.01) where f(x) = 4x 2 + 5x +2.
questions.question_html: Find the approximate value of f(2.01) where f(x) = 4x2 + 5x +2.
questions.explanation: f(x) = 4x 2 + 5x +2 Let x = 2 and {tex}\Delta x{/tex} = 0.01 Then, f(2.01) = f(x + {tex}\Delta x{/tex} ) = 4( x + {tex}\Delta x{/tex} ) 2 + 5(x + {tex}\Delta x{/tex} ) + 2 Now, {tex} \triangle y=f(x+\triangle x)-f(x){/tex} {tex} \therefore f(x+\triangle x)=f(x)+\triangle y{/tex} {tex} \approx f(x)+f'(x).\triangle x{/tex} {tex}f(2.01)\approx \left( 4{ x }^{ 2 }+5x+2 \right) +\left( 8x+5 \right) \triangle x{/tex} {tex}={ 4\left( 2 \right) }^{ 2 }+5{ \left( 2 \right) }+2+\left[ 8(2)+5 \right] .\left( 0.01 \right){/tex} {tex} =16+10+2+(21).\left( 0.01 \right) {/tex} {tex} =28+0.21{/tex} {tex}=28.21{/tex}

questions.question_text: Find the approximate value of f(5.001) where {tex}f(x)={ x }^{ 3 }-7{ x }^{ 2 }+15{/tex}
questions.question_html: Find the approximate value of f(5.001) where {tex}f(x)={ x }^{ 3 }-7{ x }^{ 2 }+15{/tex}
questions.explanation: f(x) = x 3 - 7x 2 +15 Let x = 5 and {tex}\Delta x{/tex} = 0.001 Then, f(5.001) = f(x+ {tex}\Delta x{/tex} ) = (x+ {tex}\Delta x{/tex} ) 3 - 7(x+ {tex}\Delta x{/tex} ) 2 + 15 Now, {tex}\triangle y=f(x+\triangle x)-f(x){/tex} {tex} \therefore f(x+\triangle x)=f(x)+\triangle y{/tex} {tex}\\ \approx f(x)+f'(x).\triangle x{/tex} {tex}\\ f(5.001)\approx \left( { x }^{ 3 }-7{ x }^{ 2 }+15 \right) +\left( 3{ x }^{ 2 }-14x \right) \triangle x{/tex} {tex}\\ ={ \left( 5 \right) }^{ 3 }-7{ \left( 5 \right) }^{ 2 }+15+\left[ 3{ \left( 5 \right) }^{ 2 }-14(5) \right] .\left( 0.001 \right) {/tex} {tex}\\ =125-175+15+(75-70).\left( 0.001 \right) {/tex} {tex}=-35+5.\left( 0.001 \right) =-34.995{/tex}

questions.question_text: If the radius of a sphere is measures as 7m with an error of 0.02m then find the approximate error in calculating its volume.
questions.question_html: If the radius of a sphere is measures as 7m with an error of 0.02m then find the approximate error in calculating its volume.
questions.explanation: Given , radius of the sphere is 7 m Error in the measurement of radius = {tex}\Delta r{/tex} = 0.02 m We have volume of sphere = {tex}V={ \frac { 4 }{ 3 } }\pi { r }^{ 3 }{/tex} Now, {tex}dV=\left( \frac { dV }{ dx } \right) \triangle r={ \frac { 4 }{ 3 } }\pi .3{ r }^{ 2 }\triangle r=4\pi { r }^{ 2 }.\triangle r{/tex} {tex}\ =4\pi \times 49\times 0.02=3.92\pi \;{ m }^{ 3 }{/tex}

questions.question_text: If the radius of a sphere is measured as 9 m with an error of 0.03 m, then find the approximate error in calculating the surface area
questions.question_html: If the radius of a sphere is measured as 9 m with an error of 0.03 m, then find the approximate error in calculating the surface area
questions.explanation: Given, radius of the sphere is 9 m Error in the measurement of radius = {tex}\Delta r{/tex} = 0.03 m We have Surface area of a sphere = S = {tex}4\pi r^2{/tex} Now, dS = {tex}\left( \frac { dS }{ dr } \right) \triangle r=8\pi r\triangle r=8\pi .9\times 0.03=2.16\pi \quad { m }^{ 2 }{/tex}

questions.question_text: Find the approximate change in the volume V of a cube of side x metres caused by increasing the side by 1%
questions.question_html: Find the approximate change in the volume V of a cube of side x metres caused by increasing the side by 1%
questions.explanation: Given, the side of the cube is x We have volume of a cube = V = x 3 Now, {tex} dV=\left( \frac { dV }{ dx } \right) \triangle x=3{ x }^{ 2 }\triangle x{/tex} Change in th side = {tex}\Delta x{/tex} = 0.01x Thus, the approximate change in volume = dV = 3x 2 ( 0.01 x) = 0.03 x 3 m 3 therefore, volume is increased approximately by 3 percentage.
